claim: GE Vernova spun out of GE in April 2024; three segments: Power (gas turbines + nuclear/BWRX-300 SMR + services), Wind, Electrification (grid/transformers).
notes: First clean full-year as an independent company is FY2025

claim: FY2025 revenue $38.07B (+9% YoY); operating income $1.39B (+~$0.9B YoY).
notes: Operating income is the cleaner FY2025 read; net income is distorted

claim: FY2025 net income $4.9B INCLUDES a ~$2.9B tax valuation-allowance release (non-operating, non-cash).
notes: ⚠️ One-off #1. Strip out -> operating earning power far lower than headline

claim: FY2025 free cash flow $3.7B, more than 2x FY2024's $1.7B.
notes: Genuine improvement; cleaner than net income

claim: Q1'26 revenue $9.34B (+16% YoY); net income $4.745B.

claim: Q1'26 net income includes ~$4.5B of M&A gains (primarily the Prolec GE remeasurement on buy-in) excluded from adjusted EBITDA, plus a ~$0.33–0.6B Proficy divestiture gain.
notes: ⚠️ One-off #2. Call: 'recognized $4.5B of gains from M&A transactions, primarily resulting from the acquisition of Prolec, excluded from adjusted EBITDA'; Proficy ~$600M separate. Pack used ~$4.0B Prolec + $0.33B Proficy; reconciled to $4.5B M&A total.

claim: Q1'26 free cash flow $4.8B FLATTERED by a ~$5.3B working-capital benefit (the contract-liability / customer-advance line ~+$5.6B) from turbine reservation deposits — deferred-revenue timing, not earned profit.

claim: Screener TTM net income ~$9.4B / EV-EBITDA ~85x DOUBLE-COUNTS the FY2025 tax release + Q1'26 M&A gains; true normalized earning power is far lower.

claim: Of total RPO $163.3B, services are $87.4B (54%) — multi-decade aftermarket (parts/service/upgrades) on ~7,000 installed gas turbines (~25%+ of world fleet).
notes: The services annuity is the durable rent; equipment ($75.9B) is sold near cost-to-low-margin to lock it

claim: Total RPO/backlog $163.3B at 31-Mar-2026 (equipment $75.9B / services $87.4B).
notes: Primary backlog metric

claim: Gas-turbine market structure is a 3-player heavy-duty oligopoly: GEV + Siemens Energy + Mitsubishi ≈ two-thirds of heavy-duty; GEV ~18.5% of all gas turbines (2025).
notes: GEV-specific heavy-duty share vs Siemens/Mitsubishi split is an open gap

claim: Gas turbine 100 GW under contract (44 GW firm backlog + 56 GW slot reservations), up from 83 GW QoQ on backlog.
notes: Confirmed: 44 GW backlog + 56 GW SRAs; +21 GW new agreements added in Q1

claim: Slots sold out through 2029; ~10 GW remaining cumulatively across 2029–2030; CEO targets sold-out-through-2030 by YE2026 (end 2026 with >=110 GW under contract).

claim: New 2026 Power-equipment pricing is +10–20% above Q4 2025 levels (per kW); industry path toward ~$600/kW by end-2027.

claim: Customers are pre-paying reservation deposits to hold slots (the ~$5.6B contract-liability inflow) — textbook bottleneck-owner behavior.

claim: Capacity expansion is deliberately measured (20 -> 24 GW/yr), rationing supply to optimize price/returns over volume.
notes: Management framing; supports owner-like pricing discipline

claim: Entry into heavy-duty gas turbines is effectively impossible near-term: metallurgy/IP, multi-year qualification, 3-maker oligopoly.
notes: Structural barrier; qualitative, industry-consensus

claim: Electrification backlog $42B (from $9B at YE2022); segment book-to-bill ~2.5x.
